annotations.summary: A curated awesome-list of libraries, packages, and resources for quantitative finance, covering pricing, technical indicators, backtesting, portfolio optimization, and market data across Python, R, Julia, and other languages.

Scrapy is a cross-platform web scraping framework for Python 3.10+ that extracts structured data from websites. Maintained by Zyte and community contributors, it is installed with pip and documented for building crawlers and scrapers.

Anime.js is a lightweight, multipurpose JavaScript animation library with a simple, powerful API for animating CSS properties, SVG attributes, DOM attributes, and JavaScript objects, including staggered timing, custom easing, and looped or alternating playback for web interfaces.

A community-curated, categorized list of free public APIs spanning domains such as animals, anime, security, finance, books, and business, used as a discovery reference for developers looking for third-party APIs to integrate.

YOURLS is a self-hosted, open-source URL shortener written in PHP, giving operators full control over their own shortened links, detailed click statistics, and an extensible plugin system on their own server.

FreshRSS is a lightweight, self-hostable RSS and Atom feed aggregator written in PHP, supporting multi-user access, web scraping for sites without feeds, WebSub push updates, and a Google Reader-compatible API for mobile clients.

Crawlee is a TypeScript web scraping and browser automation library for Node.js used to build reliable crawlers. It offers a single interface for HTTP and headless browser crawling with Playwright and Puppeteer, persistent request queues, integrated proxy rotation, and automatic scaling.

Creative Tim UI is a React component library built on top of shadcn/ui for building modern web applications faster. It provides pre-built, customizable components and blocks for Next.js projects, installed through a CLI or the shadcn registry, and configured with Tailwind CSS.

Botpress is an open-source platform for building chatbots and LLM-powered assistants integrated with OpenAI. This repository holds public integrations for the Botpress Hub, developer tools including a CLI, SDK, and API client, and example bots built as code.

Nakama is a scalable open-source game backend server providing user accounts, storage, social features, realtime and turn-based multiplayer, matchmaking, chat, leaderboards, and tournaments. It runs on CockroachDB or Postgres, deploys via Docker or binaries, and is extensible with Lua, TypeScript, or Go.

Community-driven collection of interactive roadmaps, guides, and educational resources that help developers plan learning paths across frontend, backend, DevOps, and other career tracks. Built in TypeScript and organized around clickable topic nodes.

Python library for downloading historical and live market data from Yahoo! Finance, returning results as pandas data structures. Commonly used to fetch stock, financial, and other market data for analysis.
A repository that stores system-administration and management-related scripts, primarily in Python, gathered together so they can be conveniently retrieved and reused.
Application for displaying and controlling an Android device from a computer, supporting screen mirroring and recording. Written in C and built on components such as FFmpeg and SDL2.

A curated list that tracks current concerning and harmful uses of artificial intelligence, covering areas such as discrimination, disinformation, and surveillance, with the goal of raising awareness of AI misuse in society.

A community-compiled collection of well-written, step-by-step guides for recreating popular technologies from scratch, covering topics such as databases, operating systems, shells, Git, emulators, programming languages, and neural networks. It is organized as a categorized list to help developers learn by building.

The Jetpack monorepo holding source code for the Jetpack WordPress plugins along with shared Composer and JavaScript packages used by them. Jetpack offers security, performance, marketing, and design tools for WordPress sites, and the repository documents build steps and a Docker-based local development workflow.

A generic Android deobfuscator that virtually executes an app to understand its behavior, then optimizes the code so it behaves identically but is easier for a human to read. It includes smalivm, a Dalvik virtual machine sandbox, and applies optimizations such as constant propagation, dead code removal, and string decryption.

A free and open-source media system that lets you manage and stream your own media collection from a dedicated server to end-user apps. Jellyfin is a cross-platform .NET alternative to the proprietary Emby and Plex, providing a server backend and API with no premium licenses, hidden features, or strings attached.

A command-line OSINT tool that hunts down social media accounts by a given username across hundreds of social networks. It can be installed through package managers such as pipx and dnf or run via Docker, helping researchers gather publicly available account information from a single query.

Code and pretrained models from OpenAI accompanying the paper Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners. This archived, as-is repository serves as a Python starting point for researchers and engineers to experiment with the GPT-2 language model.

A Python library and command-line tool that embeds and extracts blind, invisible image watermarks using a DWT-DCT-SVD algorithm. The watermark can be recovered without the original image and survives attacks such as rotation, cropping, masking, resizing, and noise.

An introductory Python training course from J.P. Morgan for business analysts and traders, delivered as Jupyter notebooks. It is a motivational demonstration of numerical computing and data visualization for people without a formal programming background, rather than a full computer science curriculum.

Google's publicly shared engineering practices documentation, capturing collective best practices developed over time. It currently centers on the Code Review Guidelines, comprising a Code Reviewer's Guide and a Change Author's Guide, and is offered so other organizations can benefit from the knowledge.

JumpServer is an open-source Privileged Access Management platform, or bastion host, built with Django. It gives DevOps and IT teams on-demand, secure browser-based access to SSH, RDP, Kubernetes, database, and RemoteApp endpoints, with auditing and access control provided through multiple connector components.

A Python library that wraps the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) and ALFRED web services. It returns economic time series as pandas Series or DataFrame objects and provides methods for handling historical data revisions, vintage dates, and full-text series search.

A Go framework for building terminal user interface applications based on The Elm Architecture, structuring programs around a model with Init, Update, and View methods. It supports inline and full-window layouts, a cell-based renderer, keyboard and mouse handling, and clipboard support.

A Python command-line launcher that organizes and installs a large collection of third-party penetration testing tools across categories such as information gathering, wireless attacks, SQL injection, phishing, forensics, and reverse engineering, with keyword search and tag filtering.

An OCR and document parsing toolkit that converts images and PDFs into structured Markdown or JSON output. It offers multilingual text recognition across more than 100 languages plus vision-language models for tables, formulas, and layout, intended to feed data into LLM and RAG pipelines.

Shell scripts and Docker images for running macOS virtual machines inside Docker containers on Linux or Windows using QEMU and KVM. It supports X11 forwarding, SSH and VNC access, USB passthrough, and Kubernetes deployment, and targets CI/CD and macOS security research.

A community-maintained repository collecting links to WWDC events, meetups, keynote watch parties, articles, videos, and tutorials from across the Apple and Swift developer community. Contributors submit pull requests to share events and learning resources, with archives of previous years.

An open source, self-hosted implementation of the Tailscale control server, written in Go. It coordinates WireGuard public keys, assigns client IP addresses, and manages a single private Tailscale network (tailnet), aimed at self-hosters, hobbyists, and small labs.

A React-based toolkit for creating videos programmatically, interactively, or with coding agents. Developers animate content with code, batch-render videos on their own infrastructure, and build video tools or editors, using React code as the source of truth.

A command-line OSINT tool that collects a dossier on a person from a username alone, checking for accounts across thousands of sites and gathering available public information from their pages. It requires no API keys and is used for reconnaissance and investigation.

A modern, simple, privacy-focused web analytics platform and open-source alternative to Google Analytics, Mixpanel, and Amplitude. Self-hosted with Node.js and PostgreSQL, it offers product and web analytics including charts, cohort analysis, and user-journey insights.

AutoScraper is a lightweight Python library for automatic web scraping. Given a URL and sample data, it learns the extraction rules and returns similar elements, then reuses the trained model on new pages to fetch matching or exact content.

LiveKit is an open-source, WebRTC-based platform for adding scalable real-time video, audio, and data to applications. Its Go server runs as a distributed SFU with JWT authentication, client and server SDKs, and single-binary, Docker, or Kubernetes deployment.

GHunt is an offensive Google framework focused on OSINT. It provides CLI modules and a Python library to gather information on email addresses, Gaia IDs, and Drive files, geolocate BSSIDs, and export results as JSON, built with a fully async design.

Depix is a proof-of-concept implementing a technique to recover plaintext from pixelized screenshots, demonstrating that certain pixelization applied to obscure text can be reversed to reveal the original content.

OpenBB Open Data Platform is an open-source toolset that integrates proprietary, licensed, and public financial data into downstream applications. It exposes data to Python, Excel, REST APIs, and MCP servers for AI agents, and ships a CLI plus a FastAPI backend.

A curated list of software services (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, and other offerings) that provide free developer tiers, scoped to services useful to system administrators and DevOps practitioners, assembled from community pull requests and reviews.

Kats is a Python toolkit from Facebook's Infrastructure Data Science team for time series analysis, offering forecasting, anomaly and change-point detection, feature extraction and embedding, and multivariate analysis in one lightweight, extensible framework.

A Node.js library for retrieving real-time market prices, indicator values, and TradingView technical analysis, supporting backtesting, replay mode, custom date ranges, and access to invite-only indicators for building trading bots and alerts.

Cal.diy is the community-driven, open-source edition of Cal.com, a self-hostable scheduling platform for managing bookings and appointments, built with Next.js, Prisma, tRPC, and the T3 stack, and intended for personal, non-production self-hosted use.

FreeMoCap is a free, open-source, hardware- and software-agnostic motion capture system with a Python graphical interface, providing minimal-cost, research-grade markerless motion capture for decentralized scientific research, education, and training.

Ansible is an agentless IT automation platform for configuration management, application deployment, cloud provisioning, ad-hoc task execution, and multi-node orchestration. It uses SSH and a human-readable language to describe infrastructure, managing remote machines in parallel without installing agents on them.

A reference collection of Warren Buffett's annual letters to the shareholders of Berkshire Hathaway spanning 1957 to 2024. The repository gathers these letters as source material on value investing associated with Buffett and his partner Charlie Munger.

Moira is a Chinese astrological chart-casting application (Qi Zheng Si Yu) repackaged here as a portable Windows build made from the original Moira-jre.exe installer with no code changes. It runs on a bundled Java runtime and is launched by clicking Moira.exe, with JRE and class paths configurable in Moira.ini.

Merlion is a Python machine learning library for time series intelligence. It offers an end-to-end framework for forecasting, anomaly detection, and change point detection on univariate and multivariate data, with standardized benchmarking, AutoML hyperparameter tuning and model selection, and post-processing that makes anomaly scores more interpretable.

curl-impersonate is a special build of curl that mimics the TLS and HTTP/2 handshakes of major browsers such as Chrome, Edge, Safari, and Firefox, so requests are not distinguished by fingerprinting. It works as a command-line tool like regular curl or as a drop-in library replacement for libcurl.

A collection of Shadowrocket proxy rule sets for iOS with strong ad-filtering. The rules are automatically generated from sources such as GFWList, EasyList and Peter Lowe's lists, deduplicated, and rebuilt and published every day.

A curated "awesome" list of libraries, packages, and resources for systematic and quantitative trading, spanning crypto, stocks, futures, options, CFDs and FX, with projects selected for domain fit and code quality.

A high-performance, self-hosted photo and video management solution with mobile and web apps for backing up, organizing, and browsing a media library, offered as an open-source alternative to Google Photos.

A fast, feature-rich, cross-platform terminal emulator written in Zig that uses platform-native UI and GPU acceleration. It also provides libghostty, a zero-dependency C and Zig library for embedding terminal functionality.
A comprehensive collection of Chinese-language deep learning study notes in Jupyter notebooks, compiled from popular video courses and covering computer vision, NLP, PyTorch, and large-model agents.

Linkwarden is a self-hosted, open-source collaborative bookmark manager for collecting, reading, annotating, and preserving webpages. It captures a screenshot, PDF, and HTML archive of each link, and offers full-text search, tags, collections, browser extensions, and native mobile apps.

A browser userscript that enables downloading images, GIFs, audio, and videos from the Telegram web app, including from chats, stories, and channels where saving media is normally restricted. It is installed through a userscript manager such as Tampermonkey or Violentmonkey.

A command-line tool written in Rust for structural search, linting, and rewriting of source code. It matches abstract syntax tree nodes rather than plain text, letting users write code-like patterns with wildcards to find and transform code across many languages using tree-sitter.

An open-source media downloader with a web frontend and API, distributed as a monorepo. Users paste a link from services such as YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, or SoundCloud to save publicly accessible video, audio, and images. It runs without ads or trackers and can be self-hosted.

An open-source, web-based control panel for managing Linux servers. It offers a graphical interface for deploying websites with SSL, managing Docker containers, images, networks, and volumes, installing applications from a marketplace, configuring firewall and security rules, and scheduling automated backups.

A headless web browser written from scratch in Zig for automation and AI agents rather than as a Chromium or WebKit fork. It exposes a Chrome DevTools Protocol interface compatible with Puppeteer and Playwright, and targets low memory use and fast execution when loading many web pages.

MuJing is a desktop application for learning English vocabulary through real context taken from movies, TV shows, and documents. It offers spelling practice, subtitle transcription, dictation tests, and a video player that shows the words being learned, plus personalized vocabulary lists generated from your own content. Runs on Windows and macOS.

FinGPT is an open-source project from the AI4Finance Foundation providing financial large language models fine-tuned with LoRA for tasks such as sentiment analysis, relation extraction, headline classification, and named-entity recognition. It publishes instruction-tuning datasets, benchmarks, and released models on Hugging Face for lightweight, low-cost adaptation to financial data.

Bruno is an open-source, offline-first API client and IDE for exploring and testing APIs, positioned as a lightweight alternative to Postman and Insomnia. It stores collections as plain-text files on your filesystem so you can version and collaborate on them with Git, and includes a CLI for running collections in automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.

Pake is a Rust and Tauri based tool that turns any webpage into a lightweight desktop application with a single command. Its installers are far smaller than Electron equivalents, and it supports macOS, Windows, and Linux with features such as keyboard shortcuts, immersive windows, drag and drop, style customization, and ad removal via a CLI or online building.

shadPS4 is an early, open-source PlayStation 4 emulator for Windows, Linux, macOS, and FreeBSD written in C++. This is the emulator core without a GUI; it can already run games such as Bloodborne, Dark Souls Remastered, and Red Dead Redemption, supports keyboard, mouse, and controller input, and can load supported PlayStation 4 firmware modules.

Ente is a fully open-source, end-to-end encrypted platform for storing data in the cloud without trusting the provider. It powers three apps: Ente Photos, a Google and Apple Photos alternative; Ente Locker for documents and credentials; and Ente Auth, a two-factor authenticator. Clients cover iOS, Android, web, Linux, macOS and Windows, and the server can be self-hosted.

prompts.chat, formerly Awesome ChatGPT Prompts, is an open-source curated library of prompt examples for AI chat models including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama and Mistral. It offers a browsable web app, downloadable datasets, a CLI, an MCP server, and a self-hosting option with custom branding, themes and authentication.

finlab.online is a multi-broker order execution framework for automated portfolio rebalancing, part of the FinLab quantitative trading package. It provides broker-specific account implementations for Fugle, Sinopac, Fubon, Masterlink, Schwab, Binance and others, a cloud-connected multi-strategy rebalancing engine, and a Jupyter notebook GUI for interactive order placement.

AstrBot is an open-source, all-in-one agent chatbot platform that connects mainstream instant-messaging apps such as QQ, Telegram, Discord and Slack to large language models. It supports multimodal conversations, MCP, knowledge bases, personas, an agent sandbox for isolated code execution, a WebUI, and over 1000 community plugins, with Docker and other deployment options.

MetaGPT is an open-source multi-agent framework that assigns roles such as product manager, architect, project manager and engineer to LLMs so they collaborate like a software company. From a one-line requirement it produces user stories, competitive analysis, requirements, data structures, APIs and code, usable via a command-line interface or as a Python library.

A frequently updated list of free public proxies covering HTTP, HTTPS, SOCKS4, and SOCKS5 protocols from many countries. Proxies are refreshed every few minutes, sorted by type and country, and made available in JSON, TXT, and CSV formats, through direct download endpoints, or via an official npm module.

A Python project that integrates ChatGPT into the Line messaging app, letting users converse with the model directly in Line. It also connects to an image-generation model for creating pictures from text prompts, supports voice input, and provides commands for registering an API token, setting a system role, and clearing history.

A web-based tool for managing, customizing, and sharing AI prompts, built with Docusaurus and React. It offers a searchable, tag-filtered library of ready-to-use prompts in many languages, personal collections, custom prompts, community sharing and voting, data export, and a companion browser extension.

A browser extension and cross-platform desktop application for text translation powered by large language model APIs. Beyond translation across many languages, it supports text polishing and summarization, streaming responses, text-to-speech, screenshot translation, and vocabulary books, and runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

A Python command-line tool that uses ChatGPT to translate documents, accepting PDF, TXT, Markdown, HTML, or RTF files or a folder of files. It produces both a direct translation and a bilingual output file, supports multithreading and configurable target languages, and includes optimized PDF parsing.

A Chinese-language LLM learning handbook for developers that translates, reproduces, and reorganizes Andrew Ng's large-model course series, covering prompt engineering, building systems with LLM APIs, RAG development, and model fine-tuning as a structured beginner-to-advanced curriculum.

A free, self-hostable studio for generating AI images and videos from over 200 models, offering text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, and lip-sync workflows, with browser, desktop-app, and local inference engine options and no built-in content filters.

A private, on-premise document intelligence platform for chatting with your own files locally so no data leaves the machine. It combines retrieval-augmented generation with hybrid semantic and keyword search, reranking, query decomposition, and an answer-verification pass, served through a web UI and REST API.

A multi-platform social media data collection tool that scrapes public posts and comments from Xiaohongshu, Douyin, Kuaishou, Bilibili, Weibo, Tieba, and Zhihu using browser automation to reuse login state. It supports keyword search, creator pages, proxy pools, a web UI, and CSV, JSON, SQLite, and MySQL storage.

A Python library that runs inference for very large language models on a single low-memory GPU by loading one transformer layer at a time, reducing VRAM needs without quantization, distillation, or pruning. It supports Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, and other open model families through one AutoModel interface.

SpacetimeDB is a relational database that also acts as a server. You upload your application logic directly into the database as a module written in Rust, C#, or other supported languages, and clients connect to it directly, executing your logic and synchronizing state to connected clients in real-time without a server in between.

A course for getting into large language models, divided into LLM Fundamentals, the LLM Scientist, and the LLM Engineer tracks. It provides roadmaps, articles, and Colab notebooks covering the mathematics, techniques, and application deployment behind building and using LLMs.

LiteLLM is an open-source AI gateway that provides a single, unified interface to call over 100 large language model providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Bedrock, and Azure using the OpenAI format. It ships as a Python SDK and a self-hosted proxy server with cost tracking, guardrails, load balancing, and logging.

A GPU-accelerated, self-contained MediaPipe plugin for TouchDesigner that runs on Mac and PC with no installation. It supports nearly all MediaPipe vision models and includes example components for loading and displaying model data from a webcam or other sources inside TouchDesigner projects.

Cognee is an open-source AI memory platform that gives AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions. It ingests data in any format and builds a self-hosted knowledge graph, combining vector embeddings, graph reasoning, and ontology generation so agents can recall and connect information with full context.

ChatDev 2.0 (DevAll) is a zero-code multi-agent platform for building and running customized LLM-powered agent systems. Users define agents, workflows, and tasks through simple configuration to orchestrate scenarios such as data visualization, 3D generation, and deep research, without writing code.

res-downloader is a cross-platform desktop tool built with Go and Wails for sniffing and downloading network media resources. It captures video, audio, images, m3u8, and live streams from platforms such as WeChat Channels, Douyin, Kuaishou, and Xiaohongshu using a configurable proxy, and runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Vizro is an open-source, low-code Python toolkit for building high-quality data visualization apps. With a few lines of configuration it assembles multi-page dashboards from components, controls, actions, and layouts, applying built-in visual design best practices, and supports customization and deployment to production.

MaxKB (Max Knowledge Brain) is an open-source platform for building enterprise-grade AI agents. It integrates retrieval-augmented generation pipelines, an agentic workflow engine with function and MCP tool-use, and is applied to intelligent customer service, corporate knowledge bases, academic research, and education.

Deep-Live-Cam is a tool for real-time face swapping and one-click video deepfakes generated from a single image. Aimed at AI-generated media production, it includes built-in checks that block processing of inappropriate media such as nudity or graphic content.

A Google Colab notebook that runs OpenAI's faster-whisper to automatically generate subtitles or transcriptions from uploaded video files. Audio is pre-processed with ffmpeg, then transcribed or translated using GPU-accelerated automatic speech recognition, with output saved as SRT or TXT files.

BAML is a statically typed programming language for building LLM agents, with a TypeScript-like syntax and Rust-like type safety. Types persist at runtime with no 'any', errors are statically analyzed, and BAML can run standalone or be called from Python, TypeScript, Go, and other languages.

Wallos is an open-source, self-hostable web application for tracking personal subscriptions and recurring expenses. It supports multiple currencies with conversion, customizable categories and themes, spending statistics, OIDC authentication, and payment notifications via email, Discord, Telegram, and webhooks, and can be deployed bare-metal or with Docker.

Open WebUI is an extensible, feature-rich, self-hosted AI platform that operates entirely offline and supports LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It includes a built-in RAG inference engine backed by multiple vector databases, granular role-based access control, plugin support, and web search integration.

An opinionated command-line tool to transcribe audio files on-device using OpenAI's Whisper, powered by Hugging Face Transformers, Optimum, and flash-attn. It supports batching and Flash Attention 2 for fast transcription on NVIDIA GPUs and Apple Silicon, with optional speaker diarization.

SuperSplat is a free, open-source editor for inspecting, editing, optimizing, and publishing 3D Gaussian Splats. Built on web technologies, it runs entirely in the browser with nothing to download or install, and offers a hosted live version.

CrewAI is an open-source Python framework for building production-ready multi-agent workflows. It provides high-level Crews for autonomous, role-based agent collaboration and low-level, event-driven Flows for precise workflow control.

Cap is an open-source alternative to Loom for recording, editing, and sharing screen recordings. It offers Instant Mode for quick share links and Studio Mode for polished local editing, with macOS and Windows apps, team workspaces, and full self-hosting via Docker.

Unsloth Studio is an application for running and training open language, audio, embedding, and vision models locally on Windows, Linux, and macOS. It supports downloading, fine-tuning, inference, tool calling, and exporting models to formats such as GGUF and safetensors.
A curated collection of assorted study materials and books gathered in PDF, PPT, and DOC formats. According to the repository description, the download links are intended to remain permanently valid.

A curated, open-source collection of over 100 runnable AI agent and RAG example applications built in Python, with step-by-step tutorials. The projects are Apache-2.0 licensed and work with multiple model providers such as Claude, Gemini, GPT, DeepSeek, Llama, and Qwen.

TimesFM is a pretrained, decoder-only time-series foundation model developed by Google Research for time-series forecasting. Distributed as a Python package with checkpoints published on Hugging Face, it supports fine-tuning and integrations with BigQuery ML and Vertex AI.

Crawl4AI is an open-source, LLM-friendly web crawler and scraper written in Python that converts web pages into clean Markdown for retrieval-augmented generation, AI agents, and data pipelines. It includes a Dockerized API server with authentication enabled by default.

ST3GG is an open-source, all-in-one steganography suite that hides data inside images, audio, documents, network packets, and other files using more than 100 encoding techniques. It runs entirely in the browser as a static site or as a Python command-line, terminal, and web interface.

Better Auth is a framework-agnostic authentication and authorization framework for TypeScript. It provides built-in features such as two-factor authentication, multi-tenant support, and single sign-on, plus a plugin ecosystem for adding advanced functionality with minimal code.

RSSBrew is a self-hosted, easy-to-deploy RSS tool written in Python. It aggregates multiple RSS feeds, applies custom filters, and generates AI-powered summaries along with daily and weekly digests of articles.

GPT-Prompt-Hub is an open-source, community-driven collection of structured prompts for large language models such as Claude, GPT, and Gemini. Each prompt is organized as a brief covering role, objective, inputs, output, constraints, and an example.

GPT-SoVITS is a Python web UI for few-shot and zero-shot voice conversion and text-to-speech. It can produce a usable TTS model from about one minute of voice data, supports cross-lingual synthesis, and includes tooling to train and fine-tune voice models.

wechatVideoDownload is a desktop tool for downloading WeChat Channels content, including videos, live-stream replays, and live streams. It can capture download links, batch-download selected clips over a chosen range, and optionally display video titles.

solidtime is a modern open-source time-tracking application for freelancers and agencies. It supports projects, tasks, clients, billable rates, multiple organizations, roles and permissions, and importing time data from tools like Toggl and Clockify. It can be self-hosted or used via its hosted cloud.

Argmax Open-Source SDK Swift is a collection of turn-key on-device inference frameworks for Apple Silicon: WhisperKit for speech-to-text with OpenAI Whisper, SpeakerKit for speaker diarization with Pyannote, and TTSKit for text-to-speech with Qwen-TTS. It includes a Swift CLI and a local inference server.

A set of Jupyter notebooks that accompany a video playlist teaching retrieval augmented generation (RAG) from scratch. It builds up an understanding of indexing, retrieval, and generation to expand a language model's knowledge base using documents retrieved from an external data source.

A collection of example MLX Swift programs for iOS and macOS, including LLM chat and text generation, MNIST training, LoRA fine-tuning, and Stable Diffusion image generation, along with command-line tools for running language and image models on Apple devices.

MinerU is a high-accuracy document parsing engine that converts PDFs and Office documents such as DOCX, PPTX, and XLSX, plus images and web pages, into structured, LLM-ready Markdown or JSON. It uses a combined vision-language and OCR engine with multi-language support and offers SDKs, a CLI, a REST API, and offline deployment.

Cherry Studio is a cross-platform desktop client for Windows, Mac, and Linux that connects to multiple LLM providers such as OpenAI, Gemini, and Anthropic, plus local models via Ollama and LM Studio. It offers 300+ preconfigured AI assistants, multi-model conversations, document and data processing, AI translation, and MCP server support.

Dawarich is a self-hostable web application that serves as an alternative to Google Timeline for tracking and visualizing location history. It shows data on an interactive map, creates and analyzes trips, supports family location sharing, imports from Google Maps Timeline, OwnTracks, GPX and GeoJSON, and reports travel statistics. It runs via Docker Compose.

Vane is a privacy-focused, self-hostable AI answering engine that runs on your own hardware. It combines web search through SearxNG with local LLMs via Ollama or cloud providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Groq to deliver answers with cited sources. Features include multiple search modes, file uploads, image and video search, and a developer API.

Dive into LLMs is a free series of hands-on programming tutorials for large language models, expanded from Shanghai Jiao Tong University course material. Delivered as Jupyter notebooks, it covers fine-tuning and deployment, prompting and chain-of-thought, knowledge editing, mathematical reasoning, text watermarking, jailbreak attacks, multimodal models, GUI agents, and RLHF alignment.

Firecrawl is an API to search, scrape, and crawl the web at scale, turning pages into clean Markdown or structured JSON for AI applications and agents. It handles proxies, rate limits, and JavaScript-heavy pages, and provides endpoints for search, scrape, crawl, map, and batch scrape, plus SDKs for Python, Node.js, Go, Rust, and other languages.

A browser-based AI image toolkit that runs entirely client-side using ONNX Runtime Web. It offers background removal, AI image upscaling, and batch processing with multiple selectable models, caching models in IndexedDB for offline reuse. No uploads or backend are involved; all processing happens locally on the user's device.

A small Python project exploring LangGraph that extracts and categorizes expenses from invoice images and stores them in a PostgreSQL database. It combines LangGraph for the processing chain, FastAPI for the API, and PostgreSQL for storage, exposing routes for categories, payment methods, and expenses.

A Chinese translation of the book Hands-On Large Language Models by Jay Alammar and Maarten Grootendorst. It provides annotated example notebooks for each chapter, covering tokens and embeddings, transformer internals, text classification, clustering, prompt engineering, semantic search and RAG, and fine-tuning, along with accompanying Chinese-language video explanations.

An open-source Python package that transforms multimodal content such as websites, PDFs, images, and YouTube videos into engaging, multilingual audio conversations using generative AI. It supports short and long-form podcasts, integrates with more than 100 LLM models and multiple text-to-speech providers, and works through a Python API or command-line interface.

An open-source, self-hostable AI command center for running a workforce of AI agents that produce real deliverables. Each session runs in an isolated cloud sandbox on its own branch, and agents commit work that reaches the main branch only through reviewed change requests. It includes connectors, skills, secrets, triggers, and shared memory.

F5-TTS is a text-to-speech system built on a diffusion transformer with ConvNeXt V2 and flow matching, released alongside an E2 TTS reproduction and a Sway Sampling inference strategy. It provides base models plus Python training and inference code for faster, fluent, and faithful speech synthesis.

LMCache is a KV cache management layer that speeds up large language model inference by storing and reusing key-value caches across requests and nodes. It integrates with serving stacks such as vLLM, supports CPU memory offloading and peer-to-peer sharing, and aims to serve LLMs faster and more cheaply.

DigitalPlat FreeDomain is a service that offers free domain names such as .dpdns.org, .us.kg, and .qzz.io that anyone can register and point to a DNS provider like Cloudflare. It aims to make the web more accessible by removing domain cost, and reports over 500,000 domains already registered.

MCP Toolbox for Databases is an open source Model Context Protocol server that connects AI agents, IDEs, and applications to databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, and Spanner. It ships prebuilt generic tools for exploring data plus a framework for building secure custom tools with structured queries.

TEN is an open-source framework for building real-time, multimodal conversational voice AI agents. Its ecosystem includes the core framework, agent examples, voice activity detection, and turn detection, with support for self-hosting via Docker and other cloud services for developers building interactive agents.

exo connects your devices into a single AI cluster to run large language models locally, splitting models across devices with topology-aware parallelism, tensor parallelism, and RDMA over Thunderbolt for lower latency. It uses MLX for inference and exposes OpenAI, Claude, and Ollama-compatible APIs plus a built-in dashboard for managing the cluster.

Scraperr is a self-hosted web scraping application that extracts data using XPath selectors without writing code. It supports queue management for multiple jobs, domain spidering, custom request headers, automatic media downloads, results visualization in tables, and export to markdown and CSV. It deploys via Docker or Helm.

A curated reference list of services that provide free access or trial credits toward API-based LLM usage. It catalogs free providers such as OpenRouter, Google AI Studio, Groq, Cohere, and others, along with providers offering trial credits, helping developers find legitimate ways to access language models via API.

A community-driven collection of runnable notebook tutorials covering Retrieval-Augmented Generation techniques, from foundational retrieval to advanced and agentic approaches. Each notebook pairs the intuition with working code and references, helping developers build more accurate, context-rich retrieval systems for language models.

Scrapling is an adaptive Python web scraping framework that scales from a single request to full crawls. Its parser learns from website changes and relocates elements automatically, its fetchers bypass common anti-bot systems, and its spider framework supports concurrent multi-session crawls with pause/resume and proxy rotation. It also ships a CLI and an MCP server for AI agents.

MiniMind is an open-source project and tutorial for training a roughly 64M-parameter language model from scratch in about two hours on a single GPU. Its core algorithms are implemented in native PyTorch and cover tokenizer training, pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, LoRA, DPO, RLAIF, tool use, adaptive thinking, distillation and MoE variants.

SurfSense is an open-source, self-hostable competitive intelligence platform for AI agents, positioned as a NotebookLM alternative with live scraping connectors. It exposes platform-native data from sources such as Reddit, YouTube and web search through a REST API and an MCP server, and keeps findings searchable in a citation-backed knowledge base.

The official ScrapeGraphAI browser extension for Chromium-based browsers and Firefox. After entering a ScrapeGraphAI API key, a user opens any webpage, types natural-language scraping instructions, and the extension extracts the requested data through the ScrapeGraphAI service, with support for custom URLs and downloadable results.

goose is an open-source, extensible AI agent from the Agentic AI Foundation that runs locally as a native desktop app, a CLI, and an API. Built in Rust, it can install, execute, edit and test code and automate workflows, working with more than 15 LLM providers and connecting to extensions through the Model Context Protocol.

Fincept Terminal is a native C++20 and Qt6 desktop application for financial analytics and investment research, with embedded Python for computation. It provides multi-asset analytics, portfolio optimization and risk metrics, over 100 data connectors, real-time and paper trading, a QuantLib quantitative suite, and configurable AI agents across multiple LLM providers.
